PRELIMINARY REPORTS OF DAMAGE ALONG OR NEAR HIGHWAY 436 AND ALONG BUFORD CREEL ROAD AND JAMES CREEL ROAD. TREES UPROOTED, POWER LINES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO HOMES, SOME SIGNIFFICANT.

A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF BUFORD CREEK ROAD AND MOVED EAST NORTHEAST CROSSING T C BRUMFIELD RD AND JAMES CREEL RD. A HOME ON BURFORD CREEK RD HAD MUCH OF ITS ROOF REMOVED.A NEARBY MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED AND A PICKUP TRUCK ROLLED. SEVERAL HOUSES ON JAMES CREEL RD HAD CONSIDERABLE ROOF DAMAGE AND A LARGE STORAGE BUIDING DESTROYED. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE ALSO UPROOTED. ESTIMATED PATH LENGTH 1.2MI WIDTH 75 YARD. A TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH OF CENTREVILLE, MS AND TRACKED NORTHWARD FOR ALMOST 2 MILES. A DOZEN OR MORE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG ITS PATH. A MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED OFF ITS FOUNDATION AND WRAPPED AROUND A LARGE TREE. THE MOBILE HOME WAS UNOCCUPIED AT THE TIME. A NUMBER OF POWER LINES AND SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED. PATH LENGTH 1.75 MILES. PATH WIDTH 125 YARDS. RATED EF1. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED 105 MPH.

DAMAGE PATH ABOUT 50 YARDS WIDE, ABOUT 0.8 MILES LONG IN THE ROGERS A-B-C ROADS AREA OFF HIGHWAY 22. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. MINOR ROOF AND EXTERIOR DAMAGE MOSTLY TO SHINGLES AND FLASHING. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR BETWEEN 744 AND 747 PM CDT. DAMAGE CONSISTENT OF EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED 90 MPH. DAMAGE PATH ABOUT 150 YARDS WIDE, ABOUT 4.4 MILES LONG. TORNADO BEGAN IN FAR EASTERN PIKE COUNTY ALONG SILVER DRIVE. TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST CROSSING NEW RIVER ROAD WHERE MOST SUBSTANTIAL TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED. TORNADO WEAKENED CROSSING STATE HIGHWAY 48 BEFORE LIFTING NEAR MESA ROAD. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT OF A TORNADO OF EF-2 STRENGTH WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 115 MPH. SEVERAL SMALLER INDIVIDUAL PATHS WERE OBSERVED IN THE TRACK INDICATING THIS WAS LIKELY A MULTI-VORTEX TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH. TREES DOWN ON SEVERAL HOMES WITH SOME HOMES HEAVILY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. THE TORNADO BEGAN AROUND SYCAMORE AVE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST AND ENDED JUST NORTH OF LA 933. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS ALONG BEECH ST AND CHRIS RD. PATH LENGTH 1.7 MI. PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS.
NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH. TREES DOWN ON HOMES AND WIRES DOWN OFF HIGHWAY 44 IN EAST SIDE OF PRAIRIEVILLE. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL FOLLOW.

PUBLIC REPORTED TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN FROM A POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO NORTHWEST OF PAINCOURTVILLE IN ASSUMPTION PARISH. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.67 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. MAX SPEED WAS 80 MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PIERRE PART RD AND CR 1004, CAUSING A POWER POLE TO LEAN. IT THEN TRAVELED NORTHEASTWARD THROUGH A FIELD CONTINUING TOWARD VIRGINIA STREET, WHERE IT TORE A CORNER PIECE OF METAL OFF OF AN OLD MOBILE HOME AND DAMAGED SOME LARGE TREE BRANCHES IN AREA THEN LIFTED.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED TORNADO DAMAGED POWER LINES AND CAUSED MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN INITIALLY JUST SOUTHWEST OF COLYELL AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ALONG SOUTH SATSUMA ROAD TO JUST NORTH OF TAYLOR ROAD. PRIMARY DAMAGE WAS DOWNED OR UPROOTED TREES AND SNAPPED LARGE BRANCHES. SOME POWER LINES WERE DOWNED. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.56 MI. MAXIMUM DAMAGE PATH WIDTH 75 YD. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED 95 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH. STORM WIDTH 75 YARDS. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE EAST BANK OF THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER IN UNION IN ST. JAMES PARISH AT THE INTERSECTION OF METHODIST STREET AND HWY 44 AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST. IT CAUSED A LINE OF SNAPPED TREES. IT ALSO DAMAGED A FARM UTILITY SHED, TEARING OFF THE ROOF OF THE SHED AND A FEW WALLS OF THE SHED. IT THEN LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER THIS AREA.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH. STORM WIDTH 75 YARDS. LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO 6 MILES NORTH OF CONVENT THAT PRODUCED DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL FOLLOW.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO 6 MILES NORTH OF CONVENT THAT PRODUCED DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH. STORM WIDTH 75 YARDS. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE EAST BANK OF THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER IN UNION IN ST. JAMES PARISH AT THE INTERSECTION OF METHODIST STREET AND HWY 44 AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST. IT CAUSED A LINE OF SNAPPED TREES. IT ALSO DAMAGED A FARM UTILITY SHED, TEARING OFF THE ROOF OF THE SHED AND A FEW WALLS OF THE SHED. IT THEN LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER THIS AREA.
MULTIPLE TREES AND TREE LIMBS SNAPPED. SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE IN COMMUNITY OF COLYELL ALONG SOUTH SATSUMA RD AND TAYLOR ROAD. TIME DETERMINED BY TDS ON RADAR.

*** 5 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF2 TORNADO NEAR SORRENTO IN ASCENSION PARISH NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 10 AND HWY 61. PEAK WIND SPEED WAS 115MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.6 MILES. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT A CHEMICAL PLANT,INJURING 5 PEOPLE AND LIFTED AN OFFICE TRAILER, SNAPPING THE METAL TIE DOWNS, AND MOVED IT 50 FEET AWAY. THE ROOF AND WALLS OF THE BUILDING WERE REMOVED. IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST FOR 1.5 MILES AND LIFTED NEAR NEW RIVER.
*** 5 INJ *** MEDIA REPORTS 5 INJURIES NEAR A PLANT ALONG LA3140 AS A RESULT OF A TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF1 TORNADO SOUTH OF KILLIAN AT THE INTERSECTION OF COOK RD AND AUSTIN ST IN LIVINGSTON PARISH. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.60 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. PEAK WIND SPEED WAS 105 MPH. IT SNAPPED AND UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES. IT THEN MOVED NORTH-NORTHEASTWARD LIFTING SHORTLY AFTER TOUCHING DOWN.

EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 1 ALONG REITH LANE. THREE WELL CONSTRUCTED WOOD STORAGE BUILDINGS HAD MAJOR ROOF AND WALL DAMAGE WITH ADJACENT CARPORT DESTROYED.NUMEROUS HARDWOOD TREES HAD LARGE BRANCHES SNAPPED.

*** 1 INJ *** A SMALL TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR RIVER ROAD NEAR GEISMAR AROUND 12 PM ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 21, 2019. A SMALL WAREHOUSE BUILDING WAS DAMAGED. ROOF MATERIALS AND PANELS WERE BLOWN OFF THE STRUCTURE AND THROWN ROUGHLY 50 YARDS AWAY. THAT DEBRIS DAMAGED ANOTHER LARGER WAREHOUSE BUILDING. THAT STRUCTURE SUSTAINED SOME DAMAGE AS SHEET METAL WAS BLOWN FROM THE OUTSIDE WALLS. DAMAGE WAS ISOLATED TO THIS AREA ONLY. ONE PERSON WAS INJURED BY PROJECTILE DEBRIS.

*** 3 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-3 TORNADO. MAXIMUM WINDS 140 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ABOUT 0.5 MILES. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN ON JUDGE ROAD, ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF MISSISSIPPI STATE HIGHWAY 24. THE TORNADO REMAINED ON THE GROUND FOR AT LEAST 20 MILES AS IT TRAVELED NORTHEAST PAST THE CITY OF SMITHDALE AND INTO LINCOLN COUNTY.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH EF-1 TORNADO. MAXIMUM WINDS 105 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH 50 YD. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON HWY 440 IN TANGIPAHOA PARISH AND MOVED NORTHEAST 1.35 MILES BEFORE LIFTING AS IT CROSSED HWY 450 IN WASHINGTON PARISH. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
